Empires & Puzzles Carta Review: The Construct Cartographer

Carta, the purple Construct healer, brings a unique 'hot potato' mechanic to Empires & Puzzles, cleansing ailments and tossing them right back at enemies. But with stats from 2024, can she still chart a course to victory in the 2026 meta? We explore her viability, emblem paths, and best synergies in this detailed review.
Carta Review: The Construct Cartographer Mapping Your Victory
Carta: The Cartographer Construct – Healing, Hasting, and Hot-Potatoing Ailments

Carta is a 5-star Dark (Purple) healer from the Construct family, originally released in April 2024 via the Covenant Summon. As a Construct, she brings the signature "Core" mechanic, which in her case is a Repair Core that boosts healing. Her claim to fame isn't just keeping your team alive; it's her ability to take the nasty status ailments your team is suffering from and "return to sender" with a random distribution mechanic. Think of her as a magical librarian who not only fixes your books but throws the overdue notices at your enemies.

Carta’s design is a fascinating blend of the Construct aesthetic—robotic, sleek, and slightly eerie—with the scholarly vibe of a cartographer. She holds a glowing scroll, likely mapping out the most efficient way to annoy the enemy team. The purple and gold color palette fits the Dark element perfectly, and the mechanical limbs give her that distinct "I was built for this, not born for it" look. It’s a solid design that communicates her role: calculated support.

As of early 2026, Carta's base stats (Power 1055, Attack 1025, Defense 1038, Health 2010) show their age. Compared to modern behemoths released in late 2025—who regularly sport 1260+ Power and stats pushing 1300/2200—Carta is decidedly "vintage." While her LB2 stats bring her closer to relevance, she is squishier than the current standard for 5-star heroes. She relies heavily on her family bonus and passives to survive the alpha-strike meta.
"Unerring Course" is a multi-faceted support skill that aims to swing momentum in your favor:
A solid chunk of healing. In the era of 2500+ HP heroes, percentage-based healing is crucial, and 45% is a respectable amount, ensuring your team steps back from the brink.
This is her signature move. Instead of just cleansing, she "lifts" the latest cleansable ailment from
allies and tosses them onto random enemies. This can be devastating if your team is covered in Defense Down or Silence. It turns the enemy's strength into their weakness. Note that it's the
This is the cherry on top. A 33% mana speed increase for 3 turns is significant, helping your slow heavy hitters charge up or your fast snipers cycle rapidly.
With +33% Mana Gen active (from her own skill): effectively drops to ~7.5 tiles for subsequent casts, making her play much faster once she gets going.
With 20% Mana troop + Family Bonus (if applicable): She can become functionally Fast (9 tiles) with the right setup.
. This is massive if the core is fully charged.
+33% is a high tier buff. Most standard mana troops give around 15-20%. This buff allows Average heroes to charge in 9 tiles (with minimal other bonuses) and Slow heroes to charge in 10.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Construct family is defined by their "Cores," which charge up as the battle progresses or when they take damage/cast skills.
The Construct family bonus provides a chance to receive less damage from special skills, which helps mitigate her lower defense stats against snipers.
This is what elevates her healing from "good" to "great." If the battle drags on, her core charges. When she fires, that extra 25%
health (overheal) acts as a second life bar. It encourages a playstyle where holding her special for a turn or two can result in a massive overheal.
A fantastic passive for a healer. It means she can't be stopped by Silence or Mindless Attack (specifically ones that block skills), ensuring she can pop her cleanse/heal when you need it most.

A bit of an odd choice for a pure support hero. While it adds some passive damage, you'd generally prefer something defensive like Damage Reduction or Defense Up to keep her alive longer. However, if she's targeted by AoE, she will punish the attacker.
Upon release in 2024, Carta was seen as a strong, if slightly niche, alternative to top-tier healers like Nautica. She offered a unique counter-play to ailment-heavy teams.

The stats gap is undeniable. Modern 2026 heroes have ~20-30% higher base stats. Her healing is percentage-based, which scales well, but her survivability is the weak link. She can be one-shot by modern snipers before she charges.
She is no longer a top-tier defense hero due to her stats. However, in

, she remains excellent. The mana generation buff is timelessly useful, and her ability to flip ailments makes her a hard counter to specific defenses that rely on DoT or debuffs. She is a "Bench" hero for top 100 players but a "Starter" for mid-game rosters.

Be careful not to overwrite a higher mana buff (if one exists) with a lower one, though +33% is usually on the high end.

If you pulled Carta, congrats! She is a versatile healer who will carry you through Map Stages and Raids. Prioritize leveling her as your main healer. Use her mana buff to make your slower, harder-hitting 4-star heroes effective.

Carta is a luxury tool. She likely won't make your main defense team. However, she is worth keeping for War depth, specifically for Rush Wars or to counter specific ailment-heavy defenses. Do not prioritize Limit Breaking her (LB2) unless you lack a modern 5-star healer; her stats simply don't justify the Alpha Aethers compared to a 2026 release.
Upon release, the community immediately compared her to Nautica. As one user noted,
"She isn't going to match up to Nautica for sure... Looks like a cheap knock off."
trading raw healing for the unique ailment flip.
Early players noticed her ailment distribution seemed buggy, seemingly targeting only one enemy. This was a hot topic, with players stating,
"I noticed that when she uses the special... she always chooses just one hero to distribute the status evils."
This behavior was later clarified/fixed to distribute randomly.
Despite the initial skepticism, those who maxed her found value.
said one player, highlighting her utility in survival.

Carta is the definition of a "tactician's healer." She doesn't just undo damage; she rewrites the rules of the engagement by speeding up your team and turning the enemy's weapons against them. While her chassis is starting to rust in the face of 2026 power creep, her kit remains mechanically sound. If you can keep her alive, she’ll map out a path to victory.
A solid B-tier hero in the modern era, elevated to A-tier in Rush formats. Keep her, use her, just don't expect her to tank hits like a modern juggernaut.
